expectations.text: Hero heading has negative letter-spacing (e.g., -0.02em, -0.03em, -2%, or similar negative value)
expectations.evidence: .hero__heading has letter-spacing: -0.03em (line 195), which is a negative value equivalent to -3%.

expectations.text: Hero heading line-height is between 1.0 and 1.25 (or 100%-125%, or a tight absolute value)
expectations.evidence: .hero__heading has line-height: 1.1 (line 194), which falls within the 1.0-1.25 range.

expectations.text: One filled/solid button and one transparent/outline/ghost button are present side by side
expectations.evidence: .btn--primary has background: var(--accent) (solid fill, line 243). .btn--ghost has background: transparent with border: 1.5px solid var(--ghost-border) (lines 259, 261). Both buttons are in .hero__cta-group with display: flex and gap: 16px (lines 219-223), placing them side by side.

expectations.text: Grouped elements have tighter spacing (e.g., 16px) than section-level gaps (e.g., 32px or larger)
expectations.evidence: CTA buttons are grouped with gap: 16px (line 222). Section-level gaps are larger: .hero__subtitle has margin-bottom: 40px before the CTA group (line 213), .hero__cta-group has margin-bottom: 72px before the trusted section (line 224), and .trusted__label has margin-bottom: 32px (line 301). The 16px button gap is tighter than the 40px-72px section-level gaps.

expectations.text: Dark mode uses lighter surface colors for cards/sections than the page background (not shadows for depth)
expectations.evidence: Dark mode defines --bg: #0f172a and --bg-alt: #1e293b (lines 39-40), where --bg-alt is lighter. However, --bg-alt is not actually applied to any card or section element in the HTML/CSS — no element uses background: var(--bg-alt) as a surface. Furthermore, dark mode still uses shadows for depth: --shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.2), --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.3), --shadow-lg: 0 8px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.35) (lines 55-57), which are actually stronger than the light mode shadows. Surface elevation through lighter colors is not implemented in practice.

expectations.text: Announcement bar uses a smaller font size than the heading and has distinct visual treatment (color, border, or background)
expectations.evidence: .announcement-bar__inner has font-size: 14px (line 156). .hero__heading has font-size: clamp(36px, 6vw, 64px) (line 192), so even at minimum 36px > 14px. Distinct visual treatment: background: var(--announcement-bg) = #eef2ff (line 146), colored text var(--announcement-text) = #4f46e5 (line 158), and a colored badge with background: var(--accent) (line 165).
